channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Strange interstellar comet speculated to be alien spacecraft is older than Solar System - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Strange interstellar comet speculated to be alien spacecraft is older than Solar System  Yahoo
  2. Interstellar comet that zoomed past Earth could be oldest and coldest object ever seen in solar system, astronomers say  CBS News
  3. Webb finds clues to ancient origin of Comet 3I/ATLAS  European Space Agency
  4. Was Methane a Signature of Life in 3I/ATLAS?  Avi Loeb – Medium
  5. Yes, interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is older than the Solar System  Big Think

item.title: A space telescope that has spent 22 years detecting the most powerful explosions in the universe is now falling toward Earth, and a startup in Arizona built its rescue craft in seven months flat — a schedule Science described as almost unheard of for a NASA m - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A space telescope that has spent 22 years detecting the most powerful explosions in the universe is now falling toward Earth, and a startup in Arizona built its rescue craft in seven months flat — a schedule Science described as almost unheard of for a NASA m  Space Daily

  4. A bold satellite rescue mission came together in record time, but will it work?  Ars Technica

item.title: An unusual strategy helps some tropical butterflies live 25 times longer than their relatives - KSL News
item.description:
1. An unusual strategy helps some tropical butterflies live 25 times longer than their relatives  KSL News

  3. Butterfly that barely ages could help unlock longevity secrets  ScienceDaily
  4. Evolution of increased longevity and slowed ageing in a genus of tropical butterfly  Nature
  5. Butterflies may hold clues to longer lives  NPR

item.title: For a decade the Pink Planet was too faint for any ground telescope to study — and when James Webb finally caught its spectrum, the best fit to the light was an atmosphere hazed with salt clouds - Space Daily
item.description:
1. For a decade the Pink Planet was too faint for any ground telescope to study — and when James Webb finally caught its spectrum, the best fit to the light was an atmosphere hazed with salt clouds  Space Daily
  2. Webb telescope finds possible salt clouds on a pink alien world  Mashable
  3. This Famously Pink Planet Is Hiding a Surprising Secret  Gizmodo
  4. "Pink Planet" is surrounded by salty clouds, researchers using Webb telescope find  CBS News
  5. Meet the exotic Pink Planet with salty clouds  EarthSky

item.title: Our sun is destined to 'kick and spit' its way across the solar system when it dies - Space
item.description:

  3. A Star's Death Throes Involves a Lot of Kicking  Caltech
  4. A star's death throes involve a lot of kicking  Phys.org

item.title: June Bootids meteor shower to peak this week - ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
item.description:
1. June Bootids meteor shower to peak this week  ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
  2. Meteor Activity Outlook for June 20-26, 2026  American Meteor Society
  3. The June Bootids have flared to dozens of meteors an hour in rare years like 1998, but most years the shower is so weak it shows almost nothing — and no outburst is forecast for 2026  Space Daily
  4. The most unpredictable meteor shower of the year peaks this week. Here's what to expect  Space
  5. June Bootids: Most chaotic meteor shower of 2026 to peak this week  The Independent
item.source: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

item.title: Europa’s surface is almost too clean. In a Solar System full of impactors, an icy moon should accumulate scars for billions of years — yet Europa has very few. The simplest explanation is also the strangest: the surface we see is young, constantly rewritten by fo - Space Daily
item.description: Europa’s surface is almost too clean. In a Solar System full of impactors, an icy moon should accumulate scars for billions of years — yet Europa has very few. The simplest explanation is also the strangest: the surface we see is young, constantly rewritten by fo  Space Daily

item.title: At its birth, Earth had no Moon. Then something perhaps the size of Mars slammed into the young planet, flinging molten debris into orbit that became the companion world now pulling our tides, steadying our seasons, and perhaps helping make Earth stable e - Space Daily

item.title: No spacecraft has ever landed in the outer solar system — except one: the Huygens probe, which parachuted through Titan's orange haze in 2005 and touched down more than a billion kilometres from Earth in cold that dropped below minus 170 degrees Cels - Space Daily
item.description: No spacecraft has ever landed in the outer solar system — except one: the Huygens probe, which parachuted through Titan's orange haze in 2005 and touched down more than a billion kilometres from Earth in cold that dropped below minus 170 degrees Cels  Space Daily

item.title: Naked mole rats almost never get cancer and routinely live more than thirty years — roughly ten times longer than any other rodent their size — and researchers in October 2025 finally identified one of the key mechanisms: a handful of tiny mutations in a singl - Space Daily

item.title: A hidden fungal network longer than the distance to the Sun wraps the roots of 70% of Earth's plants, and the first global map finds it densest not under rainforests but wild grasslands - Earth.com
item.description:
1. A hidden fungal network longer than the distance to the Sun wraps the roots of 70% of Earth's plants, and the first global map finds it densest not under rainforests but wild grasslands  Earth.com

  3. There's an Astounding Amount of Fungi Beneath Our Feet  Newser
  4. Nearly All Plants Depend on Tiny Underground Fungi. The Microbes' Vast Global Networks Were Just Mapped for the First Time  Smithsonian Magazine
  5. Scientists just finished mapping the 110-quadrillion-mile network hiding under your feet  Yahoo Creators

item.title: For 400 years, sailors crossing parts of the Indian Ocean have reported patches of sea glowing pale white from horizon to horizon, until satellite imagery finally captured the phenomenon and suggested it is likely the light of trillions of microscopic bioluminesce - Space Daily

item.title: T. rex took 40 years to reach full size, scientists find - ScienceDaily
item.description:
1. T. rex took 40 years to reach full size, scientists find  ScienceDaily
  2. T. rex may have needed up to 40 years to reach its full eight-ton size, and that slow-burn growth could help explain why the king of dinosaurs ruled for so long, as per scientists  The Times of India
  3. From birth to adulthood, T. rex took 40 years growing into an eight-ton giant, and scientists think that l  The Economic Times
  4. Tyrannosaurus Rex Didn't Reach Full Size Until Age 40, New Study Reveals  inkorr.com

item.title: Advanced AI models suffer a near-total collapse on classic psychology test as cognitive demands increase - PsyPost
item.description:

  2. AI Can Crush Complex Projects—but It Fails at This Basic Task  inc.com
  3. Even GPT-5 Failed This Human Attention Test  SciTechDaily

item.title: A spacecraft no larger than a small car, launched the year Star Wars came out, is still moving outward at 38,000 miles per hour far beyond the orbit of Pluto — and the radio signals it sends home, traveling at the speed of light, now take more than 23 hours to re - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A spacecraft no larger than a small car, launched the year Star Wars came out, is still moving outward at 38,000 miles per hour far beyond the orbit of Pluto — and the radio signals it sends home, traveling at the speed of light, now take more than 23 hours to re  Space Daily
  2. Voyager 1 has been travelling non-stop since 1977, faster than a speeding bullet every second of every day, and it has not yet reached the distance of a single light-year from Earth  Space Daily
  3. It's Official: Voyager 1 Will Break A Cosmic Record, Reaching 1 Light-Day From Earth On November 18, 2026  IFLScience
  4. The 49-Year-Old Spacecraft Still Talking From Deep Space  HackerNoon
  5. NASA Launched It In 1977, Nearly 50 Years Later, It's Still Talking To Earth | Viral News  News18

item.title: On March 13, 1989, a geomagnetic storm collapsed Quebec's entire power grid in just 90 seconds — leaving 6 million people in the cold and dark before most of them had even woken up - Space Daily
item.description:
1. On March 13, 1989, a geomagnetic storm collapsed Quebec's entire power grid in just 90 seconds — leaving 6 million people in the cold and dark before most of them had even woken up  Space Daily
  2. How to Protect Earth Against Violent Space Weather  Nautilus | Science
  3. Solar storms don't harm people directly — they induce electric currents in long metal, meaning the power lines, pipelines and undersea internet cables we've strung across the planet are exactly what they attack  Space Daily
  4. GWYNNE DYER: Solar storms: one thing less to worry about?  Windsor Star
  5. Scientists Propose Creating a Space Shield Against Solar Storms  Zamin.uz

item.title: Two marsupial species once known to science only from fossils, and thought lost for more than 6,000 years, have been confirmed alive in the rainforests of New Guinea’s Vogelkop Peninsula — a discovery made possible by old museum specimens, rare field - Space Daily
